findings.description: Double cast smell: `submission.status` is cast `as SubmissionStatus` on line 46, but it came from Prisma which already types it as `SubmissionStatus`. The cast is harmless but signals the type annotation on the model variable could be tightened (e.g. type the return of `findUnique` more precisely or use a `satisfies` narrowing).
findings.suggestion: Remove the redundant `as SubmissionStatus` cast; Prisma's generated return type already carries the enum. If inference gaps exist due to mocking, add a typed wrapper instead.

findings.description: Body is parsed with a loose cast `(await req.json()) as { status?: string }`. There is no try/catch around `req.json()`, so a malformed JSON body (e.g. empty body, non-JSON Content-Type) will throw an unhandled error and produce a 500 instead of a 400.
findings.suggestion: Wrap `await req.json()` in a try/catch and return 400 with `{ error: 'Invalid JSON body' }` on parse failure. This is consistent with Zod-validated routes elsewhere in the project.

findings.description: The `_req` parameter is typed as `Request` (native fetch API) instead of `NextRequest`. The other two routes correctly use `NextRequest`. This inconsistency is harmless today but could cause issues if any request properties specific to NextRequest are needed later.
findings.suggestion: Change `_req: Request` to `_req: NextRequest` and add `import { NextRequest } from 'next/server'` for consistency.

findings.description: Missing `export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'` pattern is present (good), but there is no error boundary around the Prisma call. An unexpected DB error would bubble up as an unhandled rejection producing a generic 500. The list and status routes have the same gap.
findings.suggestion: Wrap Prisma calls in try/catch blocks and return `{ error: 'Internal server error' }` with status 500 for consistent error responses. This also makes the error surface easier to monitor.

summary: The implementation is clean and fully aligned with the task spec. Auth uses `getServerSession(authOptions)` as required (not `getToken`). The list endpoint uses an explicit Prisma `select` that provably excludes `email`. The detail endpoint uses `include: { responses: true }` which correctly returns all responses including orphans (fieldId=null) with no filtering. The `VALID_TRANSITIONS` map covers all four enum states with the correct allowed targets. All three acceptance criteria groups are satisfied. Minor issues: missing try/catch on JSON body parsing (could produce 500 on bad input), one redundant type cast, and a minor type inconsistency (`Request` vs `NextRequest`). None of these block approval.
